stepAnswer: Solutions exist if and only if the value of k is within the range $$(-1,1)$$.
stepTitle: When solving linear trigonometric equations of the form $\sin(x) $$=$$ k$ or $\cos(x) $$=$$ k$, how do we determine if solutions exist?
answerLatex: Solutions exist if and only if the value of k is within the range $$(-1,1)$$.
choices: Solutions always exist for any value of k.
Solutions exist if and only if the value of k is within the range $$(-1,1)$$.
Solutions exist only if k is a positive value.
Solutions exist only if k is an integer.
